title: Warehouse Ventilation Rate
summary: Standard 62.1 recommends 0.3 L/(s·m²) of ventilation as a design criterion for warehouse ventilation.
evidence_quote: Table 6-1 of Standard 62.1 recommends 0.3 L/(s·m2) of ventilation as a design criterion for warehouse ventilation

title: Bus Terminal Ventilation Concern
summary: Enclosed bus loading areas present serious health and safety problems due to carbon monoxide and oxides of nitrogen emissions.
evidence_quote: Three major problems are encountered, the first and most serious of which is emission of carbon monoxide (CO) by cars and oxides of nitrogen (NOx) by buses

title: Warehouse Load Characteristics
summary: In warehouses, internal loads from lighting, people, and miscellaneous sources are low; most load is thermal transmission and infiltration.
evidence_quote: In general, internal loads from lighting, people, and miscellaneous sources are low. Most of the load is thermal transmission and infiltration.

title: Humid Climate Warehouse Humidity Control
summary: In humid climates, space humidity can become unacceptably high when cooling equipment is off due to infiltration of moist air.
evidence_quote: it is common to satisfy the space temperature (i.e., very low or no sensible cooling load), but, because of infiltration of moist air and without proper cooling (i.e., the cooling equipment is off), for space humidity to be unacceptably high.

title: Fume Hood Type Selection
summary: Variable Volume fume hoods are for moderate to highly hazardous processes with varying procedures.
evidence_quote: Variable Volume (constant face velocity). ... Application: Moderate to highly hazardous processes; varying procedures.

title: Perchloric Acid Hood Requirement
summary: Perchloric acid exhaust systems must have internal water washdown and drainage, and ductwork must be smooth, impervious, cleanable, and acid-resistant.
evidence_quote: the exhaust system must be equipped with an internal water washdown and drainage system, and the ductwork must be constructed of smooth, impervious, cleanable materials that are resistant to acid attack.

title: Cultural Heritage Zoning
summary: Spaces in similar environmental zones should be kept physically contiguous for efficient ducting and wall construction.
evidence_quote: spaces in similar environmental zones should be kept physically contiguous, with the occupied collections zone and the unoccupied storage environments adjacent to one another.

title: Loading Dock Pressurization
summary: Loading docks should ideally be positively pressurized to mitigate energy transfer and reduce condensation risk.
evidence_quote: Loading docks should ideally be positively pressurized, and shared walls with collections zones should be carefully designed to mitigate any potential energy transfer

title: Vapor Pressure Differential
summary: Vapor flow and equilibration between interior and exterior spaces continue independently based on vapor pressure, not air pressure.
evidence_quote: Vapor carried by air cannot be fully mitigated by pressurization: it slows the process by slowing air transfer, but vapor flow and equilibration between interior and exterior spaces continue independently based on vapor, rather than air, pressure.

title: Cultural Heritage Envelope Performance
summary: Where envelope performance is questionable, reassess environmental goals to minimize vapor pressure differences or consider containment alternatives.
evidence_quote: Where envelope performance is in question, the first step is to reassess environmental goals: considering either higher or lower relative humidity ranges to minimize differences in vapor pressure

title: Cultural Heritage Latent Capacity
summary: Adequate latent capacity is often disregarded with two-pipe and standard package unit designs, causing relative humidity to rise beyond safe limits.
evidence_quote: adequate latent capacity is often disregarded with two-pipe and standard package unit designs, resulting in environments that may be able to maintain temperature control, but commonly see relative humidity conditions rise beyond safe limits

title: Air Curtain Effectiveness Range
summary: Air curtains have a range of effectiveness from 60 to 90%, depending on type and application.
evidence_quote: Research (e.g., Pappas and Tassou [2003]) shows that air curtains have a range of effectiveness from 60 to 90%, depending on the type and application.

title: Air Curtain Selection Criterion
summary: Air curtain unit must cover or slightly overlap the entire opening and have a minimum velocity projection of 2 m/s at the target surface.
evidence_quote: To maximize effectiveness, the air curtain unit must at least cover or slightly overlap the entire opening, and have a minimum velocity projection of 2 m/s at the target surface
